song_desc:
[Upbeat Country-Rock]
[Male Vocals]

[Verse 1]
I kicked the red mud off my boots
State line's just a rusted sign in the wind
That old engine's humming like it knows the way
I ain't looking in the glass behind me more
No more

[Chorus]
It's just a whole lot of dust and destiny
Morning sun is hitting the hood just right
I've been bent and beaten but I will not break
Every mile is a page I am writing for myself
Writing down (Writing down)

[Verse 2]
My knuckles are scarred from the years I've lost
The radio only plays the songs that tell the truth
I got a half tank and a whole lot of nothing
And that is exactly how I like it these days
Just how I like it

[Chorus]
It's just a whole lot of dust and destiny
Morning sun is hitting the hood just right
I've been bent and beaten but I will not break
Every mile is a page I am writing for myself
Writing down (Writing down)

[Bridge]
Let the wind take every mistake I ever made
The long road doesn't ask me any questions
It just rolls out there toward the edge of the world

[Guitar Solo]

[Chorus]
It's just a whole lot of dust and destiny
Morning sun is hitting the hood just right
I've been bent and beaten but I will not break
Every mile is a page I am writing for myself
Writing down (Writing down)

[Outro]
Just me and the horizon
One mile at a time
[Guitar Fade Out]
